skill.name: ai-image-generation
skill.description: Generate and edit AI images through the inference.sh CLI using FLUX, Gemini, Grok, Seedream, Reve, and related image apps.
skill.summary: Run networked AI image generation, editing, and upscaling workflows with inference.sh image models.
skill.author: inference-shell

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command-execution hits, but most are Markdown command examples using backticks, not Ruby execution. The real risk is intentional use of Bash(infsh *) and networked inference.sh services, which is legitimate for this skill but should be disclosed. No prompt-injection text, credential exfiltration, or malicious intent was found in SKILL.md.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Execution Through infsh
medium_findings.description: The skill grants Bash access for infsh commands and shows examples that run remote image apps. This is expected for the skill, but prompts and image URLs may be sent to external services.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowed tool and examples directly show infsh command execution. The commands match the image-generation purpose, so the risk is operational rather than clearly malicious.

medium_findings.title: Related Community Skill Installation Commands
medium_findings.description: The documentation recommends npx skills add commands for related community skills. Those installations are outside this audit and should be reviewed before use.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The npx commands are explicit and install additional skills from the same source namespace. They appear informational, but they introduce supply-chain review requirements.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Expected Documentation and Example Inputs
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to inference.sh documentation, a hosted image, or placeholder image inputs. No evidence found that they receive secrets or hidden user data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible in documentation and examples. They support the declared networked image workflow, with no exfiltration logic present in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: The static high-risk weak cryptography hit on the frontmatter description is not supported by context. No cryptographic operation or weak hash use is present at that location.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is descriptive metadata listing models, capabilities, and trigger phrases. It contains no crypto API, hashing logic, or security-sensitive comparison.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bash Tool Delegation
dangerous_patterns.description: The allowed-tools declaration permits Bash execution of infsh commands. This is necessary for the skill but should remain limited to infsh.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The allowed-tools line is explicit and directly controls command access. The command scope is constrained to infsh rather than arbitrary shell commands.

dangerous_patterns.title: Networked Image Service Calls
dangerous_patterns.description: The examples run image-generation apps through inference.sh and accept remote image URLs. Users should avoid sending confidential prompts, private images, or sensitive URLs.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command examples clearly invoke remote model apps and include URL-based image inputs. The behavior is expected, but it creates data-sharing risk.

content.value_statement: Creative teams need fast access to multiple image models without memorizing every CLI option. This skill provides ready workflows for generation, editing, stitching, and upscaling through inference.sh.
actual_capabilities: Runs inference.sh image apps from Codex, Claude, or Claude Code.
Provides examples for text-to-image generation with FLUX, Gemini, Grok, Seedream, and Reve.
Shows how to list available image apps by category.
Supports documented workflows for upscaling and stitching images.
Links to inference.sh documentation for running apps and image examples.
limitations: Requires the inference.sh CLI and an authenticated account.
Sends prompts, image URLs, and generation parameters to external model providers.
Does not include local model weights or offline image generation.
Quality, latency, cost, and availability depend on each remote app.

use_cases.title: Create campaign visuals
use_cases.description: Generate polished image concepts for ads, social posts, and landing pages with several model options.

use_cases.title: Explore product mockups
use_cases.description: Create product-style photos and visual variations before committing to a formal shoot or design pass.

use_cases.title: Prototype illustration styles
use_cases.description: Test prompts across FLUX, Gemini, Grok, Seedream, and Reve to compare visual direction.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a simple image
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the ai-image-generation skill to create a square image of a modern desk setup with soft daylight.

prompt_templates.title: Compare model options
prompt_templates.scenario: Choose the best model for a creative brief
prompt_templates.prompt: Recommend an inference.sh image model for a cinematic product concept, then generate one image with that model.

prompt_templates.title: Upscale an existing image
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the upscaling workflow for this image URL and explain what input details are needed before running it.

prompt_templates.title: Build a multi-step visual workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced generation, editing, and output review
prompt_templates.prompt: Create three art-direction prompts, run the best one, then propose an upscaling or stitching follow-up if useful.

output_examples.input: Generate a product photo of a ceramic mug for a store page.
output_examples.output: The assistant selects a product-photo model, prepares a studio-lighting prompt, and returns the generated image result.

output_examples.input: Make a wide cyberpunk city image for a banner.
output_examples.output: The assistant uses a wide aspect ratio, runs a Grok or FLUX image app, and summarizes the resulting visual.

output_examples.input: Upscale this image URL for a presentation slide.
output_examples.output: The assistant checks the URL, runs the upscaling app, and provides the enhanced image output when available.

best_practices: Use clear prompts with subject, style, lighting, aspect ratio, and intended use.
Review model costs and provider terms before sending production workloads.
Avoid private images, confidential prompts, and sensitive URLs in external generation requests.
anti_patterns: Do not paste secrets, credentials, unreleased product details, or private customer data into prompts.
Do not assume related skills installed by npx have the same security profile.
Do not rely on one model output without checking quality, rights, and brand fit.

faq.question: Does this skill generate images locally?
faq.answer: No. It uses the inference.sh CLI to run remote image apps.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.

faq.question: Do I need an inference.sh account?
faq.answer: Yes. The quick start includes infsh login before running image apps.

faq.question: Can it edit or upscale images?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill documents upscaling and image workflows through available inference.sh apps.

faq.question: Is it safe to send private images?
faq.answer: No. Treat prompts and image URLs as data shared with external services.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill intentionally runs an external CLI and uses network services, but no malicious behavior was found.
